title: quercus robur var. puberula
intro: Quercus robur var. puberula, also known as the rough-leaved pedunculate oak, is a variety of oak tree native to Europe and Asia, distinguished by its hairy leaves and distinctive growth habit. The bark and leaves of this plant contain tannins, which have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The medicinal properties of Quercus robur var. puberula have been linked to several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the bark and leaves are often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat a range of conditions, from digestive issues to respiratory problems.
